items.title: Rank retrieved chunks by score
items.scenario: A retriever returns 500 chunks with similarity scores; you want the top-10 in descending order.
items.explanation: `heapq.nlargest(10, chunks, key=...)` is O(n log k); a full `sorted(..., reverse=True)[:10]` is O(n log n). With n=500 either is fine; with n=500k the heap wins by orders of magnitude.

items.title: Multi-key sort with stability
items.scenario: Sort completions by latency ascending, then by cost ascending as tiebreaker.
items.explanation: Sort by cost first, then by latency — Timsort's stability preserves the cost order within equal latencies.

items.title: Length-bucketed batching
items.scenario: Pack a training batch with sequences of similar length to minimize padding waste.
items.explanation: Sort by length, group consecutively into batches; pad each batch to its own max length instead of the global max.

items.title: External (out-of-core) sorting of logs
items.scenario: 100 GB of log lines won't fit in RAM but you need them sorted by timestamp.
items.explanation: Mergesort-based external sorting reads chunks, sorts in memory, writes runs, then k-way merges with a heap — the classic 'big data sort'.
